evals.prompt: Deploy my GitHub-based Next.js app to an existing Dokploy production project, set env vars safely, attach app.example.com with HTTPS, and verify real reachability.
evals.expected_output: Chooses MCP-first workflow, configures source/build/env/domain in safe sequence, deploys, and verifies logs + HTTP reachability.
expectations: Chooses MCP before API/CLI/SSH unless unavailable.
Avoids secret leakage while handling environment variables.
Includes post-deploy verification beyond dashboard status.

evals.prompt: MCP tools are unavailable in this session. I still need to roll out an environment-variable update and redeploy an app safely. What method do you use and how?
evals.expected_output: Selects API fallback, outlines auth/state-read/minimal-mutation/redeploy/verification flow, and keeps secrets protected.
expectations: Explicitly selects API because MCP is unavailable.

evals.prompt: Our ops box already has Dokploy CLI configured. We need to quickly restart a failing app and confirm it is healthy again.
evals.expected_output: Chooses CLI in a preconfigured environment, performs minimal restart/redeploy sequence, and verifies health + logs.
expectations: Uses CLI only after acknowledging preconfigured availability.

evals.prompt: Dokploy shows my app as running, but the public domain returns 502. Give the best troubleshooting path.
evals.expected_output: Starts with Dokploy-level inspection, then escalates to SSH/Docker/Traefik runtime checks only if needed.
expectations: Starts with panel-level diagnostics before host-level access.
Checks target port, backend health, and router mapping.
Escalates to SSH/Docker as fallback, not default.

evals.prompt: My Dokploy compose deployment loses state after redeploys, and I need backup support. What persistence strategy and rollout plan should I use?
evals.expected_output: Explains bind-mount vs named-volume tradeoffs, recommends named volumes when backups matter, and provides migration + persistence verification steps.
expectations: Differentiates bind mounts and named volumes.
Prefers named volumes for backup/restore requirements.
Warns against relying on non-persistent paths for durable state.
